report_name: Accenture Downplays Breach as Hacker '888' Claims Theft of Source Code and Cloud Keys

threat_actors.description: A threat actor who claimed responsibility for a data breach against Accenture in July 2026. The actor alleged the theft of 35GB of sensitive data, including source code, cryptographic keys, and cloud access tokens, which were offered for sale on the PwnForums cybercrime forum.
threat_actors.goals: To sell stolen data for financial profit and cause reputational damage to the victim.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor '888' claimed to have stolen Microsoft Azure Personal Access Tokens (PATs) as part of the data exfiltrated from Accenture.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor '888' allegedly stole Azure storage access keys, which were likely stored in configuration files within the exfiltrated data.

attack_patterns.name: Private Keys
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor '888' claimed to have stolen RSA and SSH cryptographic keys from Accenture's infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor '888' provided a screenshot showing the cloning of a private Azure DevOps repository, indicating exfiltration over a web service.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor '888' likely used stolen Azure Personal Access Tokens to gain valid access to Accenture's cloud accounts to access and exfiltrate data from an Azure DevOps repository.

identities.name: Accenture
identities.description: A global consulting firm that was the target of a data breach claimed by the threat actor '888'. The company acknowledged an 'isolated matter' affecting three employees but disputed the hacker's claim of a large-scale data theft.

identities.name: PwnForums
identities.description: A cybercrime forum where the threat actor '888' posted claims and evidence of the data breach against Accenture and offered the stolen data for sale.

identities.name: Microsoft Azure
identities.description: Cloud computing platform from which the threat actor '888' claimed to have stolen access tokens and storage keys belonging to Accenture.

identities.name: Azure DevOps
identities.description: A Microsoft product for software development and deployment. The threat actor '888' provided a screenshot showing the cloning of a private repository from Accenture's Azure DevOps environment.

infrastructure.name: PwnForums Cybercrime Forum
infrastructure.description: The cybercrime forum was used by the threat actor '888' to announce the breach and advertise the stolen Accenture data for sale.

indicators.name: Anomalous Azure DevOps Repository Clone
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Azure DevOps Repository Clone
id: 2b3f8a9e-1c4d-4e6f-8b7a-9c8d0e1f2a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects repository cloning events in Azure DevOps audit logs. Unusually large numbers of clones or clones from non-standard IP ranges could indicate data exfiltration, as seen in the claimed Accenture breach.
references:
  - https://www.cybersecurity-insiders.com/accenture-data-breach-hacker-claims-massive-data-theft-while-company-disputes-allegations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'Git.Clone'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects Git repository cloning events in Azure DevOps, which could signal unauthorized data exfiltration as claimed by the hacker '888'.

indicators.name: Git Clone with Embedded Personal Access Token
indicators.pattern:
title: Git Clone Command with Embedded Personal Access Token
id: 5d6e7f8a-9b0c-4d1e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects 'git clone' commands that include an embedded Personal Access Token (PAT) for authenticating to Azure DevOps. This TTP can be used to exfiltrate source code repositories using stolen credentials.
references:
  - https://www.cybersecurity-insiders.com/accenture-data-breach-hacker-claims-massive-data-theft-while-company-disputes-allegations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\git.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'clone'
      - 'https://'
      - '@dev.azure.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects the command-line execution of 'git clone' with an embedded Personal Access Token for Azure DevOps, a method for using stolen credentials to exfiltrate source code.

relationships.source_name: Git Clone with Embedded Personal Access Token
